title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · System Lens: Grep Regex
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux grep regex increment in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use fixed strings, basic or extended regular expressions, anchors, classes, context, and exit status with explicit locale assumptions.
Select cat, less, head, tail, wc, file, and od from file size, format, encoding, and inspection goal.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2 \
  | uniq -c
